home.title: Home
home.tagline: So as not to forget who you were

about.paragraph1: Udocu is a time capsule that captures, in words and images, who you are right now. An honest, warmly filmed portrait that remains when memories fade.
about.paragraph2: For now, for later, for yourself, for those who are dear to you, and for those who come after you.
about.detailParagraph1: You don't stop to think about it, but life slips through your fingers. Your story disappears if no one records it. Udocu does that for you.
about.detailParagraph2: With a few small cameras, and in a calm, warm and personal conversation that goes deeper than ordinary small talk, journalist/reporter Kurt Vandemaele captures the essence of your personality for you: your memories, your insights, your hopes, your dreams, your doubts, your fears, your life lessons… everything is covered. Your voice, your gaze, your expressions, your gestures… they are all part of the package.
about.detailParagraph3: The result is an honest and warmly filmed portrait that lives on when memories fade. Udocu is not an interview for the outside world; it is a gift to yourself and to those who care about you.

whoAmI.title: Who Am I
whoAmI.bio: I am Kurt Vandemaele, a journalist and storyteller since the age of sixteen. My career took me from local cafés to international film sets, from conversations with strangers to world-famous stars. But in the end my work always comes down to the same thing: seeing people as they are. With Udocu I help people record their story.
whoAmI.detailParagraph1: I have been a journalist for 40 years. I have worked for countless publications at home and abroad, as well as for radio and television. Over the years I have interviewed an endless number of people.
whoAmI.detailParagraph2: I love conversations that go deeper than the surface. The warmth, the doubt, the humour and the truth that lie within every life. That humanity runs like a thread through everything I make, from my written work and video series to my newest project, Udocu, in which I capture the essence of someone's life in an intimately filmed portrait.

metadata.title: udocu
